name: ESP32 Touch Pad
description: Capacitive touch detection is possible on ESP32, ESP32-S2, ESP32-S3 and ESP32-P4 processors. In ESPHome, it is configured in two parts:

config_entries.label: Debounce Count
config_entries.description:
**Set together — all of these must be set, or all left blank:** `debounce_count`, `filter_mode`, `noise_threshold`, `jitter_step`, `smooth_mode`.

Sets the debounce count; if the measured values continue to exceed the threshold for `n + 1` times, the touch sensor state changes.

config_entries.label: Denoise Cap Level
config_entries.description:
**Set together — all of these must be set, or all left blank:** `denoise_grade`, `denoise_cap_level`.

Select internal reference capacitance of denoise channel. Must be one of `L0`, `L1`, `L2`, `L3`, `L4`, `L5`, `L6` or `L7`.

config_entries.label: Denoise Grade
config_entries.description:
**Set together — all of these must be set, or all left blank:** `denoise_grade`, `denoise_cap_level`.

Sets the denoise range of the denoise channel. Determined by measuring the noise amplitude of the denoise channel. Must be one of `BIT12`, `BIT10`, `BIT8` or `BIT4`.

config_entries.label: Filter Mode
config_entries.description:
**Set together — all of these must be set, or all left blank:** `debounce_count`, `filter_mode`, `noise_threshold`, `jitter_step`, `smooth_mode`.

Sets the filter mode. Must be one of `IIR_4`, `IIR_8`, `IIR_16`, `IIR_32`, `IIR_64`, `IIR_128`, `IIR_256` (S2/S3 only) or `JITTER`.

config_entries.label: Jitter Step
config_entries.description:
**Set together — all of these must be set, or all left blank:** `debounce_count`, `filter_mode`, `noise_threshold`, `jitter_step`, `smooth_mode`.

Set jitter filter step size.

config_entries.label: Noise Threshold
config_entries.description:
**Set together — all of these must be set, or all left blank:** `debounce_count`, `filter_mode`, `noise_threshold`, `jitter_step`, `smooth_mode`.

Noise threshold coefficient. Higher = More noise resistance. The actual noise should be less than (noise coefficient * touch threshold). The coefficient is 0: 4/8; 1: 3/8; 2: 2/8; 3: 1.

config_entries.label: Smooth Mode
config_entries.description:
**Set together — all of these must be set, or all left blank:** `debounce_count`, `filter_mode`, `noise_threshold`, `jitter_step`, `smooth_mode`.

Level of filter applied on the original data against large noise interference. Must be one of `OFF`, `IIR_2`, `IIR_4` or `IIR_8`.

config_entries.label: Waterproof Guard Ring
config_entries.description:
**Set together — all of these must be set, or all left blank:** `waterproof_guard_ring`, `waterproof_shield_driver`.

Sets the touch channel to use for the guard pad. The guard pad is used to detect the large area of water covering the touch panel.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: Waterproof Shield Driver
config_entries.description:
**Set together — all of these must be set, or all left blank:** `waterproof_guard_ring`, `waterproof_shield_driver`.

Shield channel drive capability configuration; the larger the parasitic capacitance on the shielding channel, the higher the drive capability needs to be set. Must be one of `L0`, `L1`, `L2`, `L3`, `L4`, `L5`, `L6` or `L7`.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID for code generation.

config_entries.label: High Voltage Reference
config_entries.description: The high voltage reference to use for the charge cycles. One of `2.4V`, `2.5V`, `2.6V`, `2.7V`. Default is `2.7V`.

config_entries.label: Iir Filter
config_entries.description: Optionally set up an [Infinite Impulse Response](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Infinite_impulse_response) filter should be applied to all touch pads. This can increase the accuracy of the touch pads a lot, but higher values decrease the response time. A good value to start with is `10ms`. By default, the IIR filter is inactive.

config_entries.label: Low Voltage Reference
config_entries.description: The low voltage reference to use for the charge cycles. One of `0.5V`, `0.6V`, `0.7V`, `0.8V`. Default is `0.5V`.

config_entries.label: Measurement Duration
config_entries.description: Set the conversion time for all touch pads. A longer conversion time means that more charge/discharge cycles of the touch pad can be performed, therefore increasing accuracy. Default is about 8ms, the maximum amount.

config_entries.label: Setup Mode
config_entries.description: Whether debug messages with the touch pad value should

config_entries.label: Sleep Duration
config_entries.description: Set a time period denoting the amount of time the touch peripheral should sleep between measurements. This can decrease power usage but make the sensor slower. Default is about 27 milliseconds.

config_entries.label: Voltage Attenuation
config_entries.description: The voltage attenuation to use for the charge cycles. One of `1.5V`, `1V`, `0.5V`, `0V`. Default is `0V`.
